name: Seven Lakes Country Club
tagline: William Cody's architectural masterpiece — mid-century circular homes, seven lakes, and timeless Palm Springs style.
description:
Seven Lakes Country Club is one of the most architecturally significant residential communities in Palm Springs, designed by architect William F. Cody in 1963 in a distinctive circular form — each home is a self-contained round pavilion connected to the community's shared open space by a system of paths around the property's seven ornamental lakes. The result is a community that is immediately recognizable and consistently featured in mid-century architecture publications.

The community's 302 homes are set around the golf course and lakes in a manner that provides both privacy and visual connection to the community's water features. The 18-hole course provides year-round play, and the clubhouse — also Cody-designed — continues the circular architectural vocabulary of the homes.

Seven Lakes has experienced a significant resurgence of buyer interest as the mid-century modern movement has driven appreciation for Palm Springs' architectural heritage. Homes that once sold for modest prices have climbed substantially as design-conscious buyers seek authentic examples of the era's residential vision.

Escena is conveniently located near Interstate 10, Palm Springs International Airport, and downtown Palm Springs, making it practical for buyers who travel frequently or want easy access to the city's cultural amenities.

tagline: Del Webb's 55+ Bermuda Dunes community — two golf courses, resort amenities, and value pricing between Palm Desert and Indio.
description:
Sun City Bermuda Dunes is Del Webb's active adult community in the unincorporated Bermuda Dunes area of Riverside County, positioned between Palm Desert and Indio with approximately 1,200 homes and two 18-hole golf courses. Established in 1995, the community has developed the deep social infrastructure that Del Webb communities are known for — multiple clubs, fitness programs, social events, and the sense of resident ownership that makes these communities genuinely welcoming.

The two golf courses — one championship, one executive — provide variety for golfers of different skill levels and time availability. The main clubhouse houses fitness, pool, spa, tennis, and social spaces that host the community's robust programming calendar.

Bermuda Dunes' unincorporated status means slightly different tax and service structures compared to incorporated cities, but the community's central location between Palm Desert and Indio provides good access to both the western valley's cultural amenities and the eastern valley's practical services.

name: Portola Country Club
tagline: Palm Desert's classic 55+ golf community — 500 homes, affordable pricing, and 50 years of community life.
description:
Portola Country Club is one of Palm Desert's original 55+ guard-gated golf communities, established in 1973 with approximately 500 homes and an 18-hole course designed for year-round resident play. The community occupies a central Palm Desert location with access to the city's cultural amenities — El Paseo, McCallum Theatre, College of the Desert, and Eisenhower Health — that position it among the best-located affordable 55+ communities in the valley.

With over 50 years of operation, Portola has developed a resident culture that is genuinely community-minded. Golf leagues, social clubs, fitness programs, and a dining room that serves as a social anchor create an environment where new residents quickly find their circle. The community's scale — 500 homes — is large enough for variety but small enough to feel personal.

Home prices starting around $200K make Portola one of the most accessible entry points into Palm Desert guard-gated living, with the Palm Desert city address providing long-term appreciation and quality-of-life benefits that more affordable eastern valley communities cannot match.

Avondale Country Club is an established mid-market guard-gated golf community in Palm Desert, offering approximately 750 homes around an 18-hole championship course in a central Palm Desert location. Built in 1978, the community has developed the deep social infrastructure and resident relationships that make for genuine neighborhood identity.

Homes at Avondale range from attached condos starting around $350K to larger single-family residences approaching $900K, providing meaningful choice within the mid-market tier. The variety of home sizes and lot positions — fairway, mountain view, and interior — ensures that buyers at different price points within the range can find appropriate options.

The community's Palm Desert address, competitive HOA, and established amenity package make it a strong value proposition for buyers who want a full golf and country club lifestyle without reaching into the luxury tier.

name: Canyon Estates
tagline: Intimate guard-gated Palm Springs living with mountain views and mid-century character — no golf fees required.
description:
Canyon Estates is an intimate guard-gated community in south Palm Springs, situated near the Indian Canyons area with mountain views and easy access to the walking trails and natural beauty of the Santa Rosa Mountains foothills. With approximately 200 homes and no golf course, it offers the guard-gated Palm Springs address at a lower total cost than golf communities.

The community's mid-century influenced architecture, pool-centered common areas, and mountain backdrop create a quintessential Palm Springs atmosphere. Homes range from 1,400 to 2,500 square feet with covered patios, private pools (many units), and desert landscaping. The absence of golf fees and a moderate HOA make Canyon Estates appealing to buyers who want the secured community experience without paying for amenities they won't use.

The south Palm Springs location puts residents close to the Indian Canyons hiking trails, the Agua Caliente Band's cultural facilities, and a short drive to downtown Palm Springs' dining and cultural scene.

name: Mesquite Country Club

description:
Mesquite Country Club is Palm Springs' most affordable guard-gated golf community, offering 1,300 homes with an 18-hole course, pool, and full club amenities at price points that start around $200K — making it one of the most accessible entry points into gated golf living anywhere in the Coachella Valley.

With 1,300 homes, Mesquite provides the diverse, year-round social community that comes with scale. Multiple pool areas, tennis courts, a clubhouse with dining, and fitness facilities serve the resident base across the property's multiple neighborhoods. The Palm Springs address provides access to all of the city's cultural, dining, and entertainment offerings.

The community is particularly popular with buyers seeking Palm Springs lifestyle at the lowest possible total cost of ownership, as well as investors who appreciate the combination of Palm Springs brand appeal and accessible entry prices.

name: Stone Creek at La Quinta
tagline: La Quinta guard-gated living without golf fees — low HOA, contemporary homes, and the La Quinta address.
description:
Stone Creek at La Quinta is a guard-gated community that offers the La Quinta address and gated security at a competitive mid-market price without the golf membership fees that add to the monthly cost at many neighboring communities. With approximately 500 homes built in the mid-2000s, Stone Creek features contemporary single-family designs with covered patios, desert landscaping, and private pools on many lots.

The low HOA of $200–$350 per month is among the most competitive in any guard-gated community in La Quinta, making the total monthly cost of ownership significantly lower than golf communities at comparable purchase prices. For buyers who want the La Quinta lifestyle but play golf at public or semi-private courses, Stone Creek provides an excellent value proposition.

The community's location in western La Quinta provides easy access to Old Town La Quinta, the La Quinta Resort, and the full range of courses and amenities available within the city.
